title: Errors attempting to copy SOCI v2 indexed image
body:
AWS have changed SOCI indexing in V2 to now push the index into the main image

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/containers/improving-amazon-ecs-deployment-consistency-with-soci-index-manifest-v2/

Ironically because skopeo copy would not copy the SOCI index and only copy the image contents itself.

However, skopeo fails to copy to a docker-archive if the source image is a SOCI v2 indexed image with the following error

FATA[0056] creating an updated image manifest: Unknown media type during manifest conversion: "application/vnd.docker.image.rootfs.diff.tar.gzip"

Steps to recreate

1. Push an image to ECR
2. Use https://github.com/CloudSnorkel/standalone-soci-indexer to generate the SOCI v2 indexed image. Note that this will generate a new digest which contains the original image and the SOCI v2 index
3. Run skopeo copy docker://<repo>@<digest> docker-archive:///tmp/soci-v2-image.tar.gz

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Can you point at an example image, please? Or, run `skopeo inspect --raw docker://${theImage}`, and then recursively `skopeo inspect --raw docker://${theRepo}@sha256:${referencedManifest}` for all referenced manifests.

Guessing, just from the error message:
- This is an OCI image with non-OCI layer MIME types (invalid), or an ~artifact not using an artifact type — or we have a bug / ambiguity in artifact detection / handling
- _Either way_, `docker-archive:` is just not a suitable way to store non-image data. It does not preserve the original contents (e.g. decompressing compressed data), discards annotations and other fields. Use `dir:` (c/image’s non-standard format), `oci:`/`oci-archive:` (for exclusively OCI images) or a specialized mirroring tool, maybe like `oc-mirror`. (The error here happens while trying to convert an OCI manifest to the v2s2 required by `docker-archive:`)
I've confirmed that I can use the oci:// and dir:// for SOCI v2 indexed images. Thanks for the information
